summary: Manages automatic and manual backup creation, restoration, and retention of application data. Creates protective snapshots during preInit() before any other feature can modify data on disk.

actions.summary: Creates a manual on-demand backup of the current application data.

label.description: Optional human-readable label for the backup (e.g., 'before-migration').

actions.summary: Initiates the restore workflow. Dispatches a CoreFeature confirmation dialog before proceeding. The payload must contain the filename of the backup to restore.

filename.description: The filename of the backup zip to restore (e.g., 'raam-backup-20260404T150000Z.zip').

actions.summary: [INTERNAL] Executes the actual restore after user confirmation. Creates a safety backup, writes skip marker, extracts the backup, and triggers app restart.

filename.description: The filename of the backup to delete.

actions.summary: Manually triggers retention pruning. Deletes the oldest backups beyond the maxBackups threshold.
Opens the _backups directory in the native OS file explorer.
[INTERNAL] Re-scans the backup directory and updates the inventory state.
[INTERNAL] Dispatched after scanning the backup directory. Carries the updated list of backup entries.
[INTERNAL] Dispatched after a create, delete, or prune operation completes. Used to update UI state (loading indicators, toast messages).
[INTERNAL] Broadcast notification published after every public command-action completes. Follows the standardised ACTION_RESULT contract for cross-feature observability.

requestAction.description: The action name that triggered this result.
